message:
fix(fwc): remove stale #[allow(dead_code)] on live pipeline_dispatch (br-ydn1e)

Close flywheel_connectors-ydn1e filed from a /reality-check-for-project
audit pass. The attribute + comment at crates/fwc/src/main.rs:20761
said:

    #[allow(dead_code)] // Wired when host integration lands.
    fn pipeline_dispatch(args: &PipelineArgs, explicit_host: Option<&str>) -> Result<DispatchOutcome> {

That was accurate at some earlier point when the Commands::Pipeline
arm hadn't been wired, but the dispatch has been live for a while
now — grep confirms the function is called from
crates/fwc/src/main.rs:3242:

    Commands::Pipeline(args) => pipeline_dispatch(args, cli.host.as_deref())?,

The stale attribute is a navigation hazard, not a compile-time hazard:
the compiler doesn't actually warn on called functions regardless of
whether `#[allow(dead_code)]` is present, so the attribute was inert.
But any developer (or future agent) grepping for "dead_code" or
"wired when X lands" looking for unfinished surfaces gets a false
positive here.

Fix: delete the single `#[allow(dead_code)]` line and its trailing
comment. Function body, signature, and all call sites unchanged.

## Reality-check context

Beads comment captures the full audit matrix: 5 fwc subcommands
(list, history, invoke, pipeline, introspect) checked against
README.md claims. Zero subcommand-level overclaims found.
pipeline_dispatch's attribute-staleness was the single concrete
fix-up that surfaced — filed as P3 (documentation / navigation,
not behavioral).

## Beads

- Transition br-ydn1e from `open` -> `in_progress` (this commit
  addresses it; next sweep will close with reason pointing here).

- Transition flywheel_connectors-lfedd (fcp-mesh authenticated
  symbol requests bypass proof-of-need amplification gate) from
  `closed` back to `in_progress`. Bead was prematurely closed by
  the resolver commit but needs a post-landing verification pass
  to confirm the fix actually covers every branch in the admission
  path (the original close reason says "Removed the authenticated
  proof-of-need bypass in symbol request validation and added a
  regression test for authenticated requests above the proof
  threshold" — the bead author wants one more verification pass
  before closing for real).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l85v","title":"TEST: fcp-security Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-security Unit Test Suite

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for security testing and audit framework.

## Test Categories

### Capability Token Validation
- [ ] Ed25519 signature verification
- [ ] Token expiration checks
- [ ] Issuer/audience validation
- [ ] Capability scope enforcement
- [ ] Resource constraint validation

### Zone Security
- [ ] Zone boundary enforcement
- [ ] Trust level validation
- [ ] Cross-zone access denial
- [ ] Zone hierarchy traversal

### Provenance Tracking
- [ ] Taint label propagation
- [ ] Origin tracking
- [ ] Causal chain verification

### Input Validation
- [ ] SQL injection patterns
- [ ] XSS patterns
- [ ] Command injection patterns
- [ ] Path traversal patterns
- [ ] SSRF patterns

### Cryptographic Operations
- [ ] Key generation
- [ ] Signature creation/verification
- [ ] Secure random generation
- [ ] Constant-time comparisons

### Audit Logging
- [ ] Security event capture
- [ ] Tamper-evident logging
- [ ] Correlation ID tracking

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","title":"TEST: FCPS/FCPC Protocol Unit Tests (Frame Parsing, AEAD, MAC Verification)","description":"# TEST: FCPS/FCPC Protocol Unit Tests


## Goal
Prove FCPS/FCPC framing, parsing, and crypto verification are correct and bounded under adversarial inputs.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for wire protocol frame parsing and cryptographic verification.

## FCPS (Symbol Plane) Tests

### Frame Parsing
- [ ] Valid frame parses correctly
- [ ] Magic bytes validation ("FCPS")
- [ ] Version field bounds checking
- [ ] Flag combinations parsed correctly
- [ ] Symbol count matches actual symbols
- [ ] Total payload length matches content
- [ ] Zone ID hash is 32 bytes exactly
- [ ] Object ID is 32 bytes exactly

### Symbol Record Parsing
- [ ] ESI (encoding symbol identifier) extraction
- [ ] K parameter extraction
- [ ] Data length matches symbol_size
- [ ] Auth tag is 16 bytes exactly

### Datagram Envelope
- [ ] Session ID extraction (16 bytes)
- [ ] Sequence number parsing (u64 LE)
- [ ] MAC extraction (16 bytes)
- [ ] MAC verification success with correct key
- [ ] MAC verification failure with wrong key
- [ ] MAC verification failure with tampered frame
- [ ] Direction byte in MAC context

### Per-Symbol AEAD
- [ ] Sender key derivation matches spec
- [ ] Nonce derivation: frame_seq || esi
- [ ] AAD binding includes all required fields
- [ ] Decrypt success with correct key
- [ ] Decrypt failure with wrong key
- [ ] Decrypt failure with tampered ciphertext
- [ ] Decrypt failure with wrong AAD

### Frame Flags
- [ ] REQUIRES_ACK flag handling
- [ ] COMPRESSED flag handling
- [ ] ENCRYPTED flag handling
- [ ] STREAMING + STREAM_END combination
- [ ] HAS_CAP_TOKEN flag handling
- [ ] ZONE_CROSSING flag handling
- [ ] CONTROL_PLANE flag handling
- [ ] Invalid flag combinations rejected

## FCPC (Control Plane) Tests

### Frame Structure
- [ ] Canonical CBOR envelope parsing
- [ ] Object type discrimination
- [ ] Length prefix validation
- [ ] Backpressure frame parsing

### Control Objects
- [ ] ControlPlaneObject type dispatch
- [ ] Session control messages
- [ ] Capability objects
- [ ] Revocation objects
- [ ] Checkpoint objects

## DoS Resistance Tests
- [ ] Oversized frame rejected
- [ ] Invalid symbol count rejected
- [ ] Frame exceeding MTU rejected
- [ ] Memory bounds on malformed input

## Golden Vectors
Each test category needs:
- Valid frame bytes (hex)
- Expected parsed structure
- Invalid frame bytes that MUST be rejected

## Fuzz Targets
- FCPS frame parsing
- FCPC frame parsing
- Symbol record parsing
- Datagram envelope parsing

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "protocol": "fcps|fcpc",
  "operation": "parse|verify|encode",
  "frame_size": 1234,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "error_code": "optional"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All parsing paths have test coverage
- DoS protections verified
- Golden vectors match exactly
- Fuzz targets find no crashes

","notes":"Tests validated: all 72 fcp-protocol tests pass. Coverage confirmed for: FCPS frame parsing/flags/symbols, FCPC framing/AEAD/replay, MAC verification, golden vectors. Fuzz targets exist for all components. Blocked waiting for 1n78.13 completion.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindySnow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T07:55:24.993533714Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:30:23.929675939Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:30:23.929675939Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added comprehensive FCPS/FCPC protocol unit tests including DoS resistance (symbol count overflow, MTU violation, memory bounds), replay protection, and reference vector generation. All 50 golden vector tests pass (28 FCPC + 22 FCPS). Implementation dependency (1n78.13) is still in progress but tests are self-contained and complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","mvp","protocol","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-la0l1","title":"fcp-audit: AuditEntry has no signer binding (authenticity gap at crate boundary)","description":"## Finding
`AuditEntry` (crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:152) carries `actor: String` but has no signer key, no signature, no issuer attestation field. `computed_id` (lib.rs:234) is a blake3 hash of the canonical payload (integrity-against-edit, not authenticity). `verify_chain` performs no signer check.

Consequences:
1. **Forgery by any entity with append access.** Anyone who can append to the store can produce an entry claiming `actor = "agent:alice"` with no way for a verifier to refute it using the audit crate alone.
2. **Hash linkage only prevents after-the-fact tampering.** A fresh chain assembled from forged entries passes `verify_chain` cleanly: seqs are monotonic, prev pointers match, zone IDs align.
3. **`event_types::AUDIT_FORK_DETECTED` handling is incomplete.** `verify_chain` detects `audit.fork_detected` (two entries at same seq with different ids) but provides no policy — a verifier cannot tell which fork is authoritative without out-of-band signer info.

## Why this is underspecified
The crate-level rustdoc (lib.rs:1-5) says "protocol-level types for audit chains, decision receipts, event filtering, and chain verification." It does not state that authenticity is out of scope and must be enforced by a wrapping layer. A reader (or agent) building against fcp-audit can plausibly conclude that `verify_chain` is a full trust root — it is not.

## Recommendation (choose one)
- **Option A (strong):** Add `issuer_kid: KeyId` + `signature: Ed25519Signature` fields to `AuditEntry`. Have `verify_chain` accept a `key_lookup: impl Fn(&KeyId) -> Option<Ed25519VerifyingKey>` and verify each entry. Requires depending on fcp-crypto.
- **Option B (docs + fork policy):** Add crate-level rustdoc explicitly stating: "AuditEntry authenticity is out of scope for this crate. Callers MUST wrap entries with issuer signatures before persisting; `verify_chain` only verifies integrity against edits, not authenticity against forgery." Add a `ForkPolicy` enum to `VerifyReport` so fork-resolution semantics are explicit.

Option B is the viable minimum; Option A is the principled fix if authenticity should live in the crate.

## Evidence
- crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:152-185 (AuditEntry: no signature field)
- crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:234-261 (computed_id: blake3 hash, not signature)
- crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:927-1158 (verify_chain: no signer lookup)
- crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:1-5 (crate docs do not disclose the gap)
- crates/fcp-audit/Cargo.toml:14-21 (no fcp-crypto dep confirming gap is architectural)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:47:37.678875Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:19:52.063018Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T05:19:51.453377Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2805,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-la0l1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in commit bef626cf. Added issuer_kid + signature optional fields to AuditEntry, signing_bytes/sign/verify_signature methods, and verify_chain_with_signers wrapper that enforces full authenticity. Three new error variants (SignerMissing/SignatureInvalid/UnknownIssuer). Six regression tests cover sign/verify roundtrip, wrong-key rejection, tamper detection, unsigned-entry rejection, multi-entry signed chain acceptance, mid-chain unsigned rejection, and unknown-issuer rejection. Crate-level docs and verify_chain rustdoc now explicitly disclose the authenticity vs integrity distinction. Backwards-compatible: serde-default Optional fields, existing verify_chain unchanged.","created_at":"2026-04-21T05:19:52Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-lfedd","title":"fcp-mesh: authenticated symbol requests bypass proof-of-need amplification gate","description":"crates/fcp-mesh/src/symbol_request.rs:277-328
crates/fcp-mesh/src/admission.rs:913-931

Excerpt:
```
let base_limit = if is_authenticated { self.policy.max_authenticated_response } else { ... };
...
if max_response_symbols > self.policy.require_proof_of_need_above
    && !has_proof_of_need
    && !is_authenticated
```

Unused normative helper:
```
pub const fn check_amplification(..., is_authenticated: bool, has_proof_of_need: bool) -> Result<(), AdmissionError>
```

Finding: `validate_request` only requires proof-of-need for unauthenticated peers, so an authenticated requester can request a large response without any bounded missing-hint. The normative anti-amplification helper exists but is not wired into the symbol-request admission path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:35.581795Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:52:02.684224Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:52:02.683653Z","close_reason":"Removed the authenticated proof-of-need bypass in symbol request validation and added a regression test for authenticated requests above the proof threshold.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-lfedd","title":"fcp-mesh: authenticated symbol requests bypass proof-of-need amplification gate","description":"crates/fcp-mesh/src/symbol_request.rs:277-328
crates/fcp-mesh/src/admission.rs:913-931

Excerpt:
```
let base_limit = if is_authenticated { self.policy.max_authenticated_response } else { ... };
...
if max_response_symbols > self.policy.require_proof_of_need_above
    && !has_proof_of_need
    && !is_authenticated
```

Unused normative helper:
```
pub const fn check_amplification(..., is_authenticated: bool, has_proof_of_need: bool) -> Result<(), AdmissionError>
```

Finding: `validate_request` only requires proof-of-need for unauthenticated peers, so an authenticated requester can request a large response without any bounded missing-hint. The normative anti-amplification helper exists but is not wired into the symbol-request admission path.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:35.581795Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:56:58.344566Z","close_reason":"Removed the authenticated proof-of-need bypass in symbol request validation and added a regression test for authenticated requests above the proof threshold.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lib","title":"fcp.zendesk: Customer Lookup","description":"# fcp.zendesk: Customer Lookup (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable agents to retrieve customer context safely:
- look up users/requesters
- fetch basic profile metadata
- list recent tickets for context

This is read-heavy but touches PII.

## Scope
- Search users by email/name/external id.
- Fetch user profile (minimize returned PII).
- List tickets for a user (pagination).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `zendesk.users.read`
- `zendesk.tickets.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- PII hygiene:
  - never log emails/phone numbers
  - return only the minimal fields required for support workflows

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `zendesk.user.search`
  - `zendesk.user.get`
  - `zendesk.user.tickets.list`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - search pagination
  - redaction rules

## Acceptance criteria
- Customer context can be retrieved without leaking PII into logs.

Operation: zendesk.search_users. Covered by existing test suite.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:00:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lj2","title":"fcp-oauth: Shared OAuth2/OAuth1 Authentication Library","description":"# fcp-oauth: Shared OAuth2/OAuth1 Authentication Library

## Overview
Unified OAuth library supporting OAuth 2.0 (authorization code, client credentials, PKCE) and OAuth 1.0a flows for connectors requiring user authorization.

## Supported Flows

### OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code (with PKCE)
- Authorization URL generation
- State parameter for CSRF protection
- PKCE challenge/verifier
- Token exchange
- Refresh token handling

### OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials
- Service-to-service authentication
- Token caching with TTL
- Automatic refresh

### OAuth 1.0a (Twitter legacy)
- Request token acquisition
- User authorization
- Access token exchange
- HMAC-SHA1 signature generation

## Token Management
- In-memory token storage (no disk!)
- Automatic refresh before expiry
- Token validation
- Secure token handling

## Provider Support
- Google (OAuth 2.0 + PKCE)
- GitHub (OAuth 2.0)
- Twitter/X (OAuth 1.0a + OAuth 2.0)
- Slack (OAuth 2.0)
- Notion (OAuth 2.0)
- Linear (OAuth 2.0)
- Custom providers via configuration

## Security
- State parameter validation
- PKCE enforcement where supported
- Secure redirect URI handling
- Token encryption at rest

## Dependencies
- reqwest, serde_json, chrono, base64
- fcp-core (for FCP integration)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:35:45.591389925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T18:38:26.814607347Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T18:38:26.814607347Z","close_reason":"Implemented fcp-oauth crate with OAuth 2.0 (authorization code + PKCE, client credentials), OAuth 1.0a, token management, and pre-configured providers. All 25 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","title":"[Registry] Implement connector registry, signing, and cross-platform binary distribution","description":"The README describes a registry architecture with remote registries, self-hosted registries, and mesh mirrors. Before execution, FCP verifies: manifest signature, binary checksum, binary signature, platform/arch match, capability subset check, and optionally transparency log + in-toto/SLSA attestations. The supply chain verification code exists in fcp-registry and fcp-evidence, but there is no running registry, no published connector binaries, and the release.yml GitHub Actions workflow has never been demonstrated. The install/update flow (fwc install, fwc update) has never been proven against real signed artifacts. This initiative creates a minimal registry and proves the distribution flow.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"initiative","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:49.699330Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T22:01:42.213004Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T22:01:42.212640Z","close_reason":"All 5 children (lopoh.1-.5) closed: signing, local registry, fwc install/update proof, release.yml Windows ARM64 matrix, supply-chain negative tests. Initiative fully reconciled.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distribution","registry"]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-y7sow","title":"[bug] session ack verification lacks hello endpoint and suite binding","description":"Fresh-eyes audit of crates/fcp-protocol/src/session.rs found that verify_ack_attested()/MeshSessionAck::verify() validate the responder signature and attestation, but do not currently enforce that the ack reflects the original hello endpoints (ack.from == hello.to and ack.to == hello.from) or that the chosen ack.suite was actually offered in hello.suites. That means a valid responder key can sign an ack against the caller-provided hello transcript while targeting the wrong recipient or selecting an unoffered suite, and current verification still accepts it. Acceptance: reject mismatched ack endpoint reflection and reject ack.suite values not present in hello.suites, with regression tests inline in fcp-protocol.","notes":"Patched crates/fcp-protocol/src/session.rs to reject session acks that do not mirror the original hello endpoints or that select a suite the initiator never offered. Added inline regression tests for both failure modes plus error-display coverage. File-level rustfmt --check passes. Targeted rch-backed cargo test attempts selected healthy workers but both hit the known dependency-preflight fail-open blocker (RCH-E326), so I aborted the forbidden local cargo fallback immediately and left validation at non-cargo checks for this bead.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-26T00:45:10.653071Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:21:52.144457Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:21:52.144290Z","close_reason":"All 3 ack verification regression tests pass (endpoint mismatch, suite not offered, missing signature). Full fcp-protocol: 72/72 tests pass, clippy clean. Verified by SunnyMoose via RCH on 2026-03-26.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2017,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-y7sow","author":"DarkLake","text":"Patched crates/fcp-protocol/src/session.rs to reject session acks that do not mirror the original hello endpoints or that select a suite the initiator never offered. Added inline regression tests for both failure modes plus error-display coverage. File-level rustfmt --check passes. Targeted rch-backed cargo test attempts selected healthy workers but both hit the known dependency-preflight fail-open blocker (RCH-E326), so I aborted the forbidden local cargo fallback immediately and left validation at non-cargo checks for this bead.","created_at":"2026-03-26T00:47:31Z"},{"id":2019,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-y7sow","author":"jemanuel","text":"Additional verification notes on 2026-03-26: a live  run for the new  regression test selected worker  and then failed at dependency preflight with  before any remote cargo execution.  immediately attempted forbidden local fallback; I interrupted it as soon as local compilation began. Read-only SSH inspection of  on  shows a stale dirty remote checkout with , , , large sqlite artifacts, and many stray files, while  has a comparatively cleaner checkout. Existing repo comments on  also note that worker routing overrides are not currently wired and that current end-to-end blockers have shifted from retrieval overflow to dependency preflight (/). Treat the remaining gap here as /remote-state infrastructure, not evidence against the session-ack fix itself.","created_at":"2026-03-26T00:57:53Z"},{"id":2020,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-y7sow","author":"DarkLake","text":"Correction to prior note: live RCH validation for the new fcp-protocol regression test selected worker vmi1149989 and failed at dependency preflight RCH-E326 before any remote cargo execution. RCH then attempted local fallback and I interrupted it immediately. Read-only SSH inspection of /data/projects/flywheel_connectors on vmi1149989 shows a stale dirty remote checkout with .beads, .beads_recovery_20260322T034251Z, .codex, large sqlite artifacts, and many stray files; vmi1227854 is comparatively cleaner. Existing notes on flywheel_connectors-r4x01 also say worker-routing overrides are not currently wired and that end-to-end blockers have shifted from retrieval overflow to dependency preflight RCH-E326/RCH-E327. The remaining validation gap here is RCH remote-state infrastructure, not contrary evidence against the session-ack fix.","created_at":"2026-03-26T00:58:18Z"},{"id":2026,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-y7sow","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose verification on 2026-03-26: all 3 ack verification regression tests pass via RCH (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-sunnymoose): ack_verify_rejects_endpoint_mismatch, ack_verify_rejects_suite_not_offered_by_hello, ack_verify_missing_signature. Full fcp-protocol test suite: 72/72 pass. Clippy clean after adding missing Panics doc on fcps.rs:920. This bead's code fix is validated.","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:13:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ycmu","title":"[IDEA-30.3] E2E migration + validation scenario","description":"## Scenario
- Generate v1 logs, migrate to v2, validate both.

## Logging
- JSONL entries for migration steps.
## Logging
- Emit structured JSONL per `docs/STANDARD_Testing_Logging.md` with `test_name`, `module`, `phase`, `correlation_id`, and `result`.
- Persist JSONL artifacts and validate with `fcp_conformance::schemas::validate_e2e_log_jsonl`.
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-ydn1e","title":"fwc: pipeline_dispatch has stale #[allow(dead_code)] attribute despite being live","description":"## Finding

`crates/fwc/src/main.rs:20761`:
```
#[allow(dead_code)] // Wired when host integration lands.
fn pipeline_dispatch(args: &PipelineArgs, explicit_host: Option<&str>) -> Result<DispatchOutcome> {
```

The attribute claims the function is not yet wired, but it IS called from `crates/fwc/src/main.rs:3242`:
```
Commands::Pipeline(args) => pipeline_dispatch(args, cli.host.as_deref())?,
```

## Why P3 (not P2)

This is a documentation / navigation finding, not a behavioral bug. `fwc pipeline list|show|validate|run|dry-run|estimate` all dispatch correctly through this function. The `#[allow(dead_code)]` attribute is inert — the compiler would warn regardless of dispatch wiring because the function IS called — but the accompanying comment actively misleads readers who grep for "dead_code" or "wired when X lands" looking for unfinished surfaces. It was likely true at some earlier point (host integration really was incomplete) and became stale when the `Commands::Pipeline` arm landed.

## Reality-check context (bead filed during /reality-check-for-project pass)

Audit scope: 5 fwc CLI subcommands against README claims — `list`, `history`, `invoke`, `pipeline`, and (tentatively) `introspect`. Findings:

1. `fwc list` — README lines 870-874 document `--json`, `--format table/csv/markdown`. Verified: global `--format` flag at main.rs:426 (OutputFormat enum supports toon/json/jsonl/ndjson/table/csv/tsv/markdown via parse_format at main.rs:24501-24508); global `--json` flag at main.rs:431. ListArgs at main.rs:1215 has `--offline`/`--zone`/`--category`/`--include-hidden`. ✓ No overclaim.

2. `fwc history --connector X --limit N` — README line 840 documents. HistoryArgs at main.rs:2142 supports `--connector`, `--limit` (default 20), `--status`, `--since`, positional entry_id. ✓ No overclaim.

3. `fwc invoke <connector> <operation> --file payload.json` — README lines 825, 1363 document. InvokeArgs at main.rs:1861 supports `--file`, `--input`, `--stdin`, `--set`, `--zone`, `--principal`, `--idempotency-key`, `--deadline-ms`, flattened LiveAuthArgs. ✓ No overclaim.

4. `fwc pipeline list|validate|dry-run|run` — README lines 2053-2056 document. PipelineCommand enum at main.rs:2330 supports List/Show/Validate/Run/DryRun/Estimate. All six dispatch through `pipeline_dispatch` at main.rs:20762 (this bead). ✓ No subcommand overclaim; stale attribute-comment only.

5. `fwc introspect` — suggested during audit but NOT claimed anywhere in README.md. The only README reference to "introspect" is the HTTP endpoint `GET /rpc/introspect/{connector_id}` at README line 563 and a template string in `fwc new` at README line 1101. No subcommand documented, no subcommand implemented — consistent.

## Suggested fix

Remove the `#[allow(dead_code)]` attribute and the misleading comment at main.rs:20761. The function is live; the attribute serves no purpose.

## Source

Filed from a /reality-check-for-project audit pass.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ydo","title":"fcp.kubernetes: Log streaming from pods and containers","description":"# fcp.kubernetes: Log streaming from pods and containers (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe log access:
- fetch recent logs
- stream logs with follow/backpressure

Logs may contain secrets/PII; treat as tainted.

## Scope
- Get logs for a pod/container with limits:
  - tail lines
  - since seconds
- Stream logs (follow) with:
  - bounded buffering
  - cancellation
  - reconnect strategy (optional)

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `k8s.logs.read` (sensitive)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Bounded streaming:
  - max bytes per stream
  - max duration unless renewed
- Logs must never be written to disk by default.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `k8s.logs.get`
  - `k8s.logs.stream`
- Event envelope for streaming chunks with correlation IDs.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - bounds enforcement
  - backpressure behavior
  - taint propagation

## Acceptance criteria
- Log streaming is reliable and cannot cause unbounded memory growth.

Kubernetes connector now at 28 ops, 322 tests. Committed ec5c4e4d.","created_at":"2026-03-09T08:19:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yff","title":"fcp.twitter - X/Twitter API Connector","description":"# fcp.twitter - X/Twitter API Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant X/Twitter connector for social listening and (optionally) publishing/DM/streaming, with strict tainting, PII redaction, and policy-gated dangerous capabilities.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 98/100** — Highest priority Tier 1 connector.

**Why Critical**: Twitter/X is the de facto real-time information layer of the internet. Agents that can monitor, engage, and post unlock massive value for research, marketing, community management, and staying informed. This is the social listening backbone.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Quick Wins (High Value + Moderate Effort)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 1 Foundation (immediate)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,
    ConnectorArchetype::Bidirectional
]
```
- **Operational**: REST actions (search, post, etc.)
- **Streaming**: Filtered stream ingestion
- **Bidirectional**: Read + publish workflows

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::SingletonWriter
```
- **SingletonWriter**: Required for streaming cursor/dedupe state
- State tracked: `{ last_tweet_ids: HashMap<Query, Vec<TweetId>>, stream_cursor: Option<StreamPosition> }`
- Lease required for writer fencing during stream consumption

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: External API connector with OAuth credentials
- Memory isolation prevents token extraction
- Cross-platform consistent behavior

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    host_allow: vec\![
        "api.twitter.com".into(),
        "upload.twitter.com".into(),
        "stream.twitter.com".into(),
    ],
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
Twitter content is an adversarial prompt surface:
```rust
// All inbound tweets are externally sourced and tainted
incoming_tweet.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT
    | TaintFlags::PUBLIC_INPUT;

// If tweet contains URLs:
if tweet.contains_urls {
    incoming_tweet.provenance.taint |= TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;
}

// DMs are still external but paired identity:
incoming_dm.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `twitter.read.public` | Safe | Read-only public data |
| `twitter.read.account` | Safe | Read-only account data |
| `twitter.stream.read` | Safe | Read-only stream |
| `twitter.read.dms` | Risky | Private communications |
| `twitter.write.tweets` | Dangerous | Public posting |
| `twitter.write.dms` | Dangerous | Private messaging |
| `twitter.webhooks.manage` | Dangerous | Inbound vector config |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Social Listening & Research
- Monitor brand mentions and sentiment in real-time
- Track breaking news and trending topics
- Research influencers and account histories
- Analyze competitor social strategies
- Detect emerging conversations about products/topics

### Content & Engagement
- Automated posting and thread creation
- Schedule and manage content calendars
- Cross-post from other content sources (blogs, YouTube, etc.)
- Community engagement automation (likes, retweets, replies)
- DM conversations for support, sales, or community

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"What are people saying about {product} on Twitter?"
"Create a thread summarizing {topic} from these sources"
"Monitor @competitor and alert me to significant announcements"
"Post this content with optimal hashtags"
"Research {person} and summarize their Twitter presence"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Agent Mail Integration
- Coordinate tweet storms across multiple agents
- Route social mentions to appropriate agent handlers
- Thread social interactions as mail messages for audit

### CASS (Context-Aware Semantic Search)
- Index social interactions for pattern learning
- Semantic search over historical tweets/mentions
- Learn optimal engagement timing and content styles

### BV (Beads Viewer)
- Track social engagement as task outcomes
- Create beads from social monitoring alerts
- Link social campaigns to project work

### UBS (Ultimate Bug Scanner)
- Lint tweets for policy compliance before posting
- Check for sensitive content, banned words, legal issues
- Validate thread formatting and length limits

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **Implement both v1.1 and v2 APIs** for maximum coverage
  - v1.1: DMs, some legacy endpoints
  - v2: Tweets, users, lists, spaces (modern)
- Handle rate limits gracefully with **token rotation**
- Support **webhook subscriptions** for real-time events (Account Activity API)

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client with connection pooling
- `tokio-tungstenite` — WebSocket for streaming
- `oauth1-request` / `oauth2` — OAuth handling
- `serde_json` — JSON parsing

### Rate Limiting Strategy
- Parse `x-rate-limit-*` headers on every response
- Implement per-endpoint rate tracking
- Never retry blindly; respect `Retry-After`
- Support credential rotation for higher throughput

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:social:twitter:personal` | Full personal account access | All read/write/DM |
| `z:social:twitter:brand` | Managed brand posting | Write tweets only |
| `z:social:twitter:monitor` | Read-only monitoring | Search, read timelines |

### Single-Zone Binding
- One connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId`
- Cannot cross-zone post from monitoring instance
- Separate instances for separate accounts/zones

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — REST actions (search, post, etc.)
- `streaming` — Filtered stream ingestion
- `bidirectional` — Read + publish workflows

---

## Threat Model

**Twitter content is an adversarial prompt surface.**

### Hard Requirements
- Inbound content is tainted as external input by default
- Strong redaction: no OAuth secrets in logs; avoid logging raw tweet/DM bodies
- Never execute code from tweet content
- Rate limit to prevent abuse/spam detection

### Dangerous Operations (Require Explicit Capability)
- `twitter.write.tweets` — Can post public content
- `twitter.write.dms` — High sensitivity; private communications
- `twitter.webhooks.manage` — Can receive inbound data

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `api.twitter.com:443`
- `upload.twitter.com:443`
- `stream.twitter.com:443` (if streaming enabled)

### Credential Injection
- OAuth 1.0a tokens via `CredentialId` (consumer key/secret, access token/secret)
- OAuth 2.0 bearer token via `CredentialId` (for app-only auth)
- Never write credentials to disk

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `twitter.read.public` — Search and public tweets
- `twitter.read.account` — Timelines, mentions
- `twitter.read.dms` — Direct message inbox
- `twitter.read.lists` — List memberships/content

### Write Operations (Dangerous)
- `twitter.write.tweets` — Create, reply, thread, delete
- `twitter.write.retweets` — Retweet, unretweet
- `twitter.write.likes` — Like, unlike
- `twitter.write.dms` — Send direct messages

### Streaming
- `twitter.stream.read` — Filtered stream ingestion
- `twitter.stream.rules` — Manage stream filter rules

### Administrative (Dangerous)
- `twitter.webhooks.manage` — Account Activity API setup

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Read Operations
- `search` — Search recent/archive tweets
- `user.lookup` — Get user profiles by ID/username
- `user.timeline` — Get users tweets
- `user.mentions` — Get mentions of authenticated user
- `tweet.get` — Get specific tweets by ID
- `trends.get` — Get trending topics

### Write Operations
- `tweet.create` — Post a new tweet
- `tweet.reply` — Reply to existing tweet
- `thread.create` — Create multi-tweet thread
- `tweet.delete` — Delete own tweet
- `retweet` / `unretweet`
- `like` / `unlike`
- `dm.send` — Send direct message

### Streaming Operations
- `stream.connect` — Connect to filtered stream
- `stream.rules.add` — Add filter rules
- `stream.rules.delete` — Remove filter rules

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Rate limit parsing and backoff logic
- OAuth signature generation
- Tweet/thread formatting
- Taint labeling of inbound content

### Integration Tests
- wiremock-based API simulation
- Streaming connection simulation (SSE)
- Error response handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates default deny + NetworkConstraints
- Verifies tainting of external content
- Confirms no credential leakage in logs

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- Deterministic tests; no live Twitter/X calls in unit tests
- Usage metrics emitted correctly
- Rate limiting works correctly under load
- Both v1.1 and v2 API operations functional
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yff.1","title":"fcp.twitter: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.twitter: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the Twitter/X connector manifest contract:
- archetypes (`operational`, `streaming`, `bidirectional`)

- capability families + per-operation caps
- NetworkConstraints allowlist for API + streaming

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete manifest spec stored here.
- Good/bad manifest examples exist for static compliance.
